channel.title: Exxon on The Guyana Brief
channel.description: Recent content in Exxon on The Guyana Brief

item.title: Patriots Portfolio — March 24, 2026: Oil, Courts, and the Business of Everything
item.description: This week in Guyana's business and economic landscape: Exxon's production push, rising oil prices, the NRF, and what the Mohameds saga means for business confidence.

item.title: Tuesday's Guyana Brief: Wheelbarrows, Oil Greed, and the Death of a Newspaper
item.description: The Mohameds went to the CCJ, Exxon wants more oil, and somewhere in the background, a newspaper quietly died. Tuesday's 5-minute Guyana news circus.

item.title: Uncle Ramesh's Take: Cash In Hand, Oil On The Rise, And Some People Just Can't Accept A Good Thing
item.description: Uncle Ramesh from Toronto is very pleased with the cash grant, very confident in Exxon, and has some thoughts about people who pay court debts in wheelbarrows.

item.title: YouTube Scripts – March 24, 2026: Wheelbarrows, Oil, and the CCJ
item.description: Two HeyGen-ready YouTube scripts for Tuesday March 24, 2026 — 60-second quick brief and 4-minute full episode.

item.title: ☕ Monday Brief: Former Finance Minister Admits Exxon Exploited Us, Venezuela Border Watch, and Pay-To-Party Culture
item.description: Winston Jordan finally admits what everyone knew, PM Phillips says troops are ready for anything Venezuelan, and birthday parties now cost admission.

item.title: 🇬🇾 Uncle Ramesh Responds: Jordan Finally Talking Truth, Border Security Strong, and Mash Coming!
item.description: Uncle Ramesh sets the record straight on who really signed that oil contract, praises border vigilance, and gets ready for Republic Day.
